description: Drop the redundant scalar attributes.agent / attributes.project that Claude Code reflects from OTEL_RESOURCE_ATTRIBUTES onto every event record (canonical clawker identity lives at resource.agent / resource.project; dashboards filter on those). The reflected scalar attributes.agent collides with the attributes.agent object mapping (Claude Code also emits agent.name / skill.name / plugin.name / marketplace.name attribution on api_request/api_error per its OTel schema), which dropped 100% of claude-code logs. Removing the reflected scalars frees attributes.agent for Claude Code's own object-shaped attribution. Then reshape Claude Code's split attributes.prompt emissions into a single object field. Some events (user_prompt) ship attributes.prompt as a scalar string AND a sibling attributes.prompt.id; others (hook_execution_*, tool_decision) ship only attributes.prompt.id. OpenSearch auto-expands the dotted key into a nested object, which collides with the scalar prompt mapping. Move the scalar to attributes.prompt.value and keep attributes.prompt.id under the same object. Paired with attributes.prompt: {properties: {id: keyword, value: text}} on the claude-code index template.

script.source: if (ctx.attributes == null) { return; } ctx.attributes.remove('agent'); ctx.attributes.remove('project'); def p = ctx.attributes.get('prompt'); if (p instanceof String) { def id = ctx.attributes.remove('prompt.id'); ctx.attributes.remove('prompt'); Map obj = new HashMap(); obj.put('value', p); if (id != null) { obj.put('id', id); } ctx.attributes.put('prompt', obj); }
